name: dynamic_rule_override
definition:
@kbench.task(name="dynamic_rule_override", version=2)
def dynamic_rule_override(llm, prompt: str, answer: str,
                           type: str, difficulty: int) -> bool:
    response = llm.prompt(prompt)
    resp = response.strip().lower()
    ans = answer.strip().lower()

    # Special handling for instruction-override inhibition items
    if ans == "wrong":
        # Model should give a number that is NOT 6 (for "3+3 wrong answer")
        # Extract last number to avoid penalizing "not 6, I'll say 7" reasoning
        nums = re.findall(r'\b\d+\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(nums) and nums[-1] != "6"
    elif ans == "wrong_10":
        # Model should give a number that is NOT 10 (for "5*2 wrong answer")
        nums = re.findall(r'\b\d+\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(nums) and nums[-1] != "10"
    elif ans == "exclude_common":
        # Should list fruits but NOT apple/banana/orange; must list something
        has_forbidden = bool(re.search(r'\b(?:apple|banana|orange)s?\b', resp))
        # Check model listed at least one word (not just refusal)
        has_content = len(resp.split()) >= 2
        is_correct = not has_forbidden and has_content
    elif ans == "exclude_common_animals":
        # Should list animals but NOT dog/cat/bird; must list something
        has_forbidden = bool(re.search(r'\b(?:dog|cat|bird)s?\b', resp))
        has_content = len(resp.split()) >= 2
        is_correct = not has_forbidden and has_content
    elif ans == "any_other_color":
        excluded = {"red", "blue", "green", "yellow"}
        words = set(re.findall(r'\b\w+\b', resp))
        other_colors = {"purple", "pink", "orange", "brown", "black", "white",
                       "gray", "grey", "violet", "indigo", "teal", "cyan",
                       "magenta", "maroon", "turquoise", "beige", "coral",
                       "crimson", "lavender", "salmon", "ivory", "khaki",
                       "chartreuse", "aqua", "navy", "olive", "tan", "gold",
                       "silver", "scarlet", "burgundy", "copper", "peach",
                       "plum", "lilac", "periwinkle", "mauve", "rust"}
        is_correct = bool(words & other_colors) and not bool(words & excluded)
    elif ans == "any_other_planet":
        words = set(re.findall(r'\b\w+\b', resp))
        valid_planets = {"mercury", "venus", "saturn", "uranus", "neptune", "pluto"}
        excluded_planets = {"earth", "mars", "jupiter"}
        is_correct = bool(words & valid_planets) and not bool(words & excluded_planets)
    elif ans == "3_words":
        # Extract last line or text after colon as the actual answer
        answer_text = resp.split('\n')[-1].strip()
        if ':' in answer_text:
            answer_text = answer_text.split(':', 1)[1].strip()
        word_count = len(answer_text.split())
        is_correct = word_count == 3
    elif ans == "2_words":
        answer_text = resp.split('\n')[-1].strip()
        if ':' in answer_text:
            answer_text = answer_text.split(':', 1)[1].strip()
        word_count = len(answer_text.split())
        is_correct = word_count == 2
    elif ans == "not_55":
        # Use last number to avoid CoT/reasoning number extraction
        nums = re.findall(r'\d+', resp)
        if nums:
            n = int(nums[-1])
            is_correct = 50 < n < 60 and n != 55
        else:
            is_correct = False
    elif ans == "not_25":
        nums = re.findall(r'\d+', resp)
        if nums:
            n = int(nums[-1])
            is_correct = 20 < n < 30 and n != 25
        else:
            is_correct = False

    # Proactive interference: check if new mapping value appears (not old one)
    elif type == "proactive_interference":
        # Answer is a single word/number — check last non-empty line / after colon
        lines = [l.strip() for l in resp.strip().split('\n') if l.strip()]
        answer_text = lines[-1] if lines else resp.strip()
        if ':' in answer_text:
            answer_text = answer_text.split(':', 1)[1].strip()
        # Word boundary match for the answer
        is_correct = bool(re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(ans) + r'\b', answer_text))

    # Backward inhibition: 3 comma-separated numeric answers
    elif type == "backward_inhibition":
        expected = [n.strip() for n in ans.split(',')]
        # Strip numbered list prefixes (e.g., "1. 8\n2. 14" or "1) 8") before extracting answers
        cleaned_resp = re.sub(r'(?m)^\s*\d+[.):\-]\s*', '', resp)
        resp_nums = re.findall(r'-?\d+', cleaned_resp)
        # Take last N numbers if model produced CoT reasoning
        if len(resp_nums) > len(expected):
            resp_nums = resp_nums[-len(expected):]
        is_correct = resp_nums == expected

    # Conflict Adaptation: 2 comma-separated color names
    elif type == "conflict_adaptation":
        expected_colors = [c.strip().lower() for c in ans.split(',')]
        # Extract color words from response
        color_set = {"red", "blue", "green", "yellow", "purple", "orange"}
        resp_words = re.findall(r'\b\w+\b', resp)
        resp_colors = [w for w in resp_words if w in color_set]
        # Take last 2 colors (final answer after CoT)
        if len(resp_colors) > 2:
            resp_colors = resp_colors[-2:]
        is_correct = resp_colors == expected_colors

    # Trail Making Test B: alternating number-letter sequence
    elif type == "trail_making":
        # Extract numbers and letters from the LAST line (skip preamble)
        last_lines = resp.strip().split('\n')
        answer_text = last_lines[-1] if last_lines else resp
        # If last line has separators (-, >, ,), use it; otherwise try full response
        if re.search(r'[\->,]', answer_text):
            resp_tokens = re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]+|\d+', answer_text)
        else:
            resp_tokens = re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]+|\d+', resp)
        expected_tokens = re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]+|\d+', ans)
        # Normalize: uppercase letters
        resp_tokens = [t.upper() if t.isalpha() else t for t in resp_tokens]
        expected_tokens = [t.upper() if t.isalpha() else t for t in expected_tokens]
        # Score: ≥80% of tokens must match in order
        matches = sum(1 for a, b in zip(expected_tokens, resp_tokens) if a == b)
        is_correct = (len(resp_tokens) >= len(expected_tokens) and
                      matches >= max(1, int(len(expected_tokens) * 0.8)))

    # Go/no-go sequence scoring: compare GO/STOP token sequences
    elif type == "go_nogo":
        expected_tokens = [t.upper() for t in ans.split()]
        # Extract GO/STOP tokens from response
        resp_tokens = re.findall(r'\b(go|stop)\b', resp, re.IGNORECASE)
        resp_tokens = [t.upper() for t in resp_tokens]
        # Score: ≥80% of tokens must match in order
        if len(resp_tokens) >= len(expected_tokens):
            resp_tokens = resp_tokens[-len(expected_tokens):]
        matches = sum(1 for a, b in zip(expected_tokens, resp_tokens) if a == b)
        is_correct = (len(resp_tokens) == len(expected_tokens) and
                      matches >= max(1, int(len(expected_tokens) * 0.8)))

    # Task-switching sequence scoring: compare answer token sequences
    elif type == "task_switching":
        # Reconstruct "not round" tokens in both expected and response
        def _reconstruct_tokens(word_list):
            tokens = []
            i = 0
            while i < len(word_list):
                if word_list[i] == "not" and i + 1 < len(word_list) and word_list[i+1] == "round":
                    tokens.append("not round")
                    i += 2
                elif word_list[i] == "not":
                    i += 1  # skip orphan "not"
                else:
                    tokens.append(word_list[i])
                    i += 1
            return tokens

        expected_tokens = _reconstruct_tokens(ans.split())
        # Extract answer words from response
        resp_words = re.findall(r'[a-z]+', resp)
        valid_answers = {"greater", "less", "odd", "even", "vowel", "consonant",
                        "first", "second", "bigger", "smaller", "round", "not"}
        resp_filtered = [w for w in resp_words if w in valid_answers]
        final_tokens = _reconstruct_tokens(resp_filtered)
        # Take last N tokens matching expected length (skip CoT preamble)
        if len(final_tokens) >= len(expected_tokens):
            final_tokens = final_tokens[-len(expected_tokens):]
        matches = sum(1 for a, b in zip(expected_tokens, final_tokens) if a == b)
        is_correct = (len(final_tokens) == len(expected_tokens) and
                      matches >= max(1, int(len(expected_tokens) * 0.8)))

    # Dual-task scoring: must get BOTH parts correct
    elif type == "dual_task":
        if ans.startswith("a="):
            # Arithmetic dual-stream: extract A=<num> B=<num>
            match_a = re.search(r'A\s*=\s*(-?\d+)', resp, re.IGNORECASE)
            match_b = re.search(r'B\s*=\s*(-?\d+)', resp, re.IGNORECASE)
            exp = re.match(r'a=(-?\d+)\s+b=(-?\d+)', ans)
            if match_a and match_b and exp:
                is_correct = (match_a.group(1) == exp.group(1) and
                             match_b.group(1) == exp.group(2))
            else:
                is_correct = False
        else:
            # Letter+number mixed: extract Letters=<...> Sum=<num>
            exp_match = re.match(r'letters=(.*?)\s+sum=(\d+)', ans)
            if not exp_match:
                is_correct = False
            else:
                exp_letters = exp_match.group(1).split()
                exp_sum = exp_match.group(2)
                # Extract between "Letters=" and "Sum=" markers
                between = re.search(r'letters?\s*[=:]\s*(.*?)\s+sum\s*[=:]', resp, re.IGNORECASE)
                sum_match = re.search(r'sum\s*[=:]\s*(\d+)', resp, re.IGNORECASE)
                if between and sum_match:
                    resp_letters = between.group(1).strip().upper().split()
                    is_correct = (resp_letters == [l.upper() for l in exp_letters] and
                                 sum_match.group(1) == exp_sum)
                else:
                    is_correct = False

    # Single letter answers (WCST perseveration) — handle BEFORE generic matching
    elif len(ans) == 1 and ans in 'abcd':
        # Look for explicit choice patterns first
        choice = re.search(
            r'(?:answer\s*(?:is)?:?\s*|->?\s*|choose\s*|option\s*|group\s*|^\s*)([abcd])\s*[.!,)}\]]*\s*$',
            resp, re.MULTILINE)
        if not choice:
            # Look for standalone choice letter (not "a" as article)
            choice = re.search(r'(?:^|\.\s+|\n)\s*([abcd])\s*[.!]?\s*$', resp)
        if not choice:
            # Last word that is a single letter a-d, but skip article "a" before common words
            cleaned_resp = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|new|great)\b', '', resp)
            words = cleaned_resp.split()
            for w in reversed(words):
                w_clean = w.strip('.,!?:;)(')
                if len(w_clean) == 1 and w_clean in 'abcd':
                    class _Match:
                        def __init__(self, val): self._val = val
                        def group(self, n=1): return self._val
                    choice = _Match(w_clean)
                    break
        is_correct = bool(choice) and choice.group(1) == ans

    # Digit sequences (working memory span) — only for working_memory type
    elif type == "working_memory" and ' ' in ans and all(c.isdigit() or c == ' ' for c in ans):
        ans_digits = ans.split()
        # Extract digit SEQUENCES or spaced digits, skipping explanatory numbers
        # Look for the answer pattern in the response
        digit_seq_match = re.search(r'(?:^|[:\-]\s*)(\d[\s,]+(?:\d[\s,]*)+\d)', resp)
        if digit_seq_match:
            resp_digits = re.findall(r'\d+', digit_seq_match.group(1))
        else:
            # Fallback: extract digits from the LAST line (models typically put answer last)
            last_line = resp.strip().split('\n')[-1]
            resp_digits = re.findall(r'\d+', last_line)
        # Take last N digits to skip CoT explanations
        if len(resp_digits) > len(ans_digits):
            resp_digits = resp_digits[-len(ans_digits):]
        is_correct = resp_digits == ans_digits

    # Prospective Memory: check both arithmetic AND cue-word insertion
    elif type == "prospective_memory":
        expected_parts = [p.strip() for p in ans.split(';')]
        # Extract numbered answers from response
        resp_lines = resp.strip().split('\n')
        resp_answers = []
        for line in resp_lines:
            # Strip numbered prefix like "1." or "1)"
            cleaned = re.sub(r'^\s*\d+[.)]\s*', '', line.strip())
            if cleaned:
                resp_answers.append(cleaned.lower().strip())
        # Match expected parts
        correct_count = 0
        for i, expected in enumerate(expected_parts):
            expected_lower = expected.lower().strip()
            if i < len(resp_answers):
                resp_ans = resp_answers[i]
                # Check if the cue word and number match
                # Expected might be "FLAG 51" or just "51"
                exp_parts = expected_lower.split()
                if len(exp_parts) == 2:  # cue_word + number
                    # Both cue word and number must be present (word-boundary for numbers)
                    if (exp_parts[0] in resp_ans and
                        re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(exp_parts[1]) + r'\b', resp_ans)):
                        correct_count += 1
                elif len(exp_parts) == 1:  # just number
                    if re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(exp_parts[0]) + r'\b', resp_ans):
                        correct_count += 1
        # Require ≥70% correct (both math + prospective cues)
        is_correct = correct_count >= max(1, int(len(expected_parts) * 0.7))

    # Hayling suppression: word must NOT be in the blocklist
    elif type == "hayling_suppression":
        blocklist = set(w.strip().lower() for w in ans.split('|'))
        # Extract last word from response
        words = re.findall(r'\b[a-z]+\b', resp)
        word = words[-1] if words else ""
        # Must be a real word (>1 char) and NOT in blocklist
        is_correct = len(word) > 1 and word not in blocklist

    # Iowa Gambling Task: multi-turn deck selection with feedback
    elif type == "iowa_gambling":
        # Parse loss schedules from answer (ans is lowercased, so keys are lowercase)
        schedules = {}
        for part in ans.split(';'):
            deck, losses_str = part.split(':')
            schedules[deck.upper()] = [int(l) for l in losses_str.split(',')]
        rewards = {'A': 100, 'B': 100, 'C': 50, 'D': 50}
        deck_picks = {d: 0 for d in 'ABCD'}
        total_earnings = 0
        # Already got Turn 1 response
        for turn in range(10):
            if turn == 0:
                turn_resp = response.strip().upper()
            else:
                turn_resp = llm.prompt(
                    f"Turn {turn + 1} of 10. Your total so far: ${total_earnings}.\n"
                    f"Choose a deck (A, B, C, or D).").strip().upper()
            # Extract deck choice
            deck_letters = re.findall(r'\b([A-D])\b', turn_resp)
            choice = deck_letters[-1] if deck_letters else 'A'
            pick_idx = deck_picks[choice] % len(schedules[choice])
            reward = rewards[choice]
            loss = schedules[choice][pick_idx]
            net = reward + loss
            total_earnings += net
            deck_picks[choice] += 1
            # Send feedback (except after last turn)
            if turn < 9:
                llm.prompt(
                    f"You drew from Deck {choice}.\n"
                    f"Reward: +${reward}. {'Penalty: $' + str(loss) if loss < 0 else 'No penalty.'}\n"
                    f"Net this turn: ${'+' if net >= 0 else ''}{net}. Running total: ${total_earnings}.")
        # Score: advantage decks (C/D) vs disadvantage (A/B) in last 5 turns
        good_picks = sum(1 for d in 'CD' for _ in range(deck_picks[d]))
        # Compute from actual last-5 decisions... simplified: use total C+D picks ratio
        cd_ratio = (deck_picks['C'] + deck_picks['D']) / 10
        is_correct = cd_ratio >= 0.5  # Learned to prefer C/D

    elif type == "directed_forgetting":
        # Bjork (1970): answer format "word1:YES|word2:NO|..."
        expected_pairs = ans.split("|")
        resp_lines = resp.strip().split('\n')
        # Search for each expected word in response lines (not index-based)
        correct_count = 0
        for expected in expected_pairs:
            word, expected_yn = expected.split(":")
            expected_yn = expected_yn.strip().lower()
            word_lower = word.strip().lower()
            for line in resp_lines:
                if word_lower in line.lower():
                    yns = re.findall(r'\b(yes|no)\b', line.lower())
                    if yns and yns[-1] == expected_yn:
                        correct_count += 1
                    break
        is_correct = correct_count >= len(expected_pairs) - 1  # Allow 1 error

    elif type == "cognitive_estimation":
        # Shallice & Evans (1978): answer must fall within acceptable range
        low_str, high_str = ans.split("|")
        low_bound, high_bound = float(low_str), float(high_str)
        numbers = re.findall(r'-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?', resp)
        if numbers:
            estimate = float(numbers[-1])
            is_correct = low_bound <= estimate <= high_bound
        else:
            is_correct = False

    elif type == "operation_span":
        # Turner & Engle (1989): recall words + verify math
        word_str, math_str = ans.split("|")
        expected_words = [w.strip().lower() for w in word_str.split(",")]
        expected_math = [m.strip() for m in math_str.split(",")]
        # Extract words from WORDS: line
        words_match = re.search(r'words?:\s*(.+?)(?:\n|$)', resp, re.IGNORECASE)
        recalled = [w.strip().lower() for w in words_match.group(1).split(",")] if words_match else []
        # Extract math from MATH: line
        math_match = re.search(r'math:\s*(.+?)(?:\n|$)', resp, re.IGNORECASE)
        math_answers = re.findall(r'-?\d+', math_match.group(1)) if math_match else []
        # Score: correct words in correct position
        word_correct = sum(1 for a, b in zip(expected_words, recalled) if a == b)
        # Check math accuracy (>=85%)
        math_correct = sum(1 for a, b in zip(expected_math, math_answers) if a == b)
        math_engaged = len(expected_math) == 0 or math_correct / len(expected_math) >= 0.85
        is_correct = math_engaged and word_correct >= max(1, len(expected_words) - 1)

    elif type == "self_ordered_pointing":
        # Petrides & Milner (1982): items in alphabetical order
        expected = [w.strip().lower() for w in ans.split(",")]
        recalled = [w.strip().lower() for w in resp.split(",")]
        if len(recalled) < len(expected):
            lines = resp.split("\n")
            for ln in lines:
                if "," in ln:
                    recalled = [w.strip().lower() for w in ln.split(",")]
                    break
        is_correct = recalled == expected

    elif type == "delay_discounting":
        # Kirby et al. (1999): A/B choice
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?=\w{2,})', '', resp)
        letters = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        if not letters:
            letters = re.findall(r'\b([AB])\b', response.strip())
            letters = [l.lower() for l in letters]
        is_correct = bool(letters) and letters[-1] == ans

    elif type == "verbal_fluency":
        # Thurstone (1938): check word constraints
        parts = ans.split("|")
        n_required = int(parts[0])
        check_type = parts[1]
        param = parts[2]
        # Extract words from response
        words = [w.strip().lower().rstrip('.!,;:') for w in re.split(r'[,\n]+', resp) if w.strip()]
        words = [w for w in words if len(w) > 0 and ' ' not in w]
        unique_words = list(dict.fromkeys(words))  # preserve order, remove dupes
        if len(unique_words) < n_required:
            is_correct = False
        elif check_type == "starts_with":
            valid = [w for w in unique_words if w.startswith(param)]
            is_correct = len(valid) >= n_required
        elif check_type == "category":
            valid_set = set(param.split(","))
            valid = [w for w in unique_words if w in valid_set]
            is_correct = len(valid) >= n_required
        else:  # excludes
            valid = [w for w in unique_words if param not in w]
            is_correct = len(valid) >= n_required

    elif type == "random_generation":
        # Baddeley (1966): check if sequence is validly random-ish
        parts = ans.split("|")
        n_required = int(parts[0])
        low = int(parts[1])
        high = int(parts[2])
        # Extract digits from response
        nums = re.findall(r'\d+', resp)
        nums = [int(n) for n in nums if low <= int(n) <= high]
        if len(nums) < n_required:
            is_correct = False
        else:
            nums = nums[:n_required]
            # Check for monotonic runs (3+ ascending or descending)
            runs = 0
            for i in range(len(nums) - 2):
                if nums[i] < nums[i+1] < nums[i+2] or nums[i] > nums[i+1] > nums[i+2]:
                    runs += 1
            # Check for repetitions (same digit 3+ consecutive)
            repeats = 0
            for i in range(len(nums) - 2):
                if nums[i] == nums[i+1] == nums[i+2]:
                    repeats += 1
            # Score: valid range + enough digits + no excessive patterns
            is_correct = runs <= n_required // 4 and repeats == 0

    elif type == "set_shifting":
        # Berg (1948): extract card number (1-4)
        numbers = re.findall(r'\b([1-4])\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(numbers) and numbers[-1] == ans

    elif type == "stop_signal":
        # Logan & Cowan (1984): count non-empty classification lines
        expected_count = int(ans)
        # Count lines that contain a classification keyword
        lines = [l.strip() for l in resp.strip().split('\n') if l.strip()]
        # Filter to lines containing classification words
        class_lines = [l for l in lines if re.search(r'\b(animal|fruit|color|not)\b', l, re.IGNORECASE)]
        is_correct = len(class_lines) == expected_count

    elif type == "part_set_cueing":
        # Slamecka (1968): word recall
        is_correct = ans.lower() in resp.lower()

    elif type == "response_compatibility":
        # Deininger & Fitts (1954): incompatible mappings
        is_correct = ans in resp

    elif type == "gratification_delay":
        # Mischel (1972): delayed gratification A/B choice
        choices = re.findall(r'\b([AB])\b', resp.upper())
        is_correct = bool(choices) and choices[-1] == ans.upper()

    elif type == "interference_control":
        # Hasher & Zacks (1988): ignore irrelevant info
        # Try exact match first, then number match
        if re.match(r'^-?\d+$', ans):
            numbers = re.findall(r'\b(\d+)\b', resp)
            is_correct = bool(numbers) and numbers[-1] == ans
        else:
            is_correct = ans.lower() in resp.lower()

    elif type == "cognitive_offloading":
        # Risko & Gilbert (2016): recall with/without external store
        if re.match(r'^-?\d+$', ans):
            numbers = re.findall(r'\b(\d+)\b', resp)
            is_correct = bool(numbers) and numbers[-1] == ans
        else:
            is_correct = ans.lower() in resp.lower()

    elif type == "mental_set":
        # Luchins (1942): water jar problem
        numbers = re.findall(r'\b(\d+)\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(numbers) and numbers[-1] == ans

    elif type == "retrieval_induced_forgetting":
        # Anderson et al. (1994): recall unpracticed items
        is_correct = bool(re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(ans) + r'\b', resp))

    elif type == "action_sequence_planning":
        # Botvinick & Plaut (2004): next step in sequence
        is_correct = bool(re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(ans) + r'\b', resp))

    elif type == "temporal_discounting":
        # Green & Myerson (2004): immediate vs delayed reward
        id_matches = re.findall(r'\b(immediate|delayed)\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(id_matches) and id_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "loss_aversion":
        # Kahneman & Tversky (1979): accept/reject gamble by EV
        ar_matches = re.findall(r'\b(accept|reject)\b', resp)
        is_correct = bool(ar_matches) and ar_matches[-1] == ans

    elif type == "rule_abstraction":
        # Ashby & Maddox (1998): classify by learned rule
        # Filter article "a" before extraction
        filtered = re.sub(r'\ba\s+(?:more|very|real|likely|clear|good|bad|bit|lot|few|word|new|person|because|is|has|was|item|belongs|category)\b', '', resp)
        ab_matches = re.findall(r'\b([ab])\b', filtered)
        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

        is_correct = bool(ab_matches) and ab_matches[-1] == ans

    else:
        # Standard answer checking — use word-boundary matching
        is_correct = bool(re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(ans) + r'\b', resp))

        # For numeric answers, use the LAST number (final answer after CoT)
        if not is_correct and re.match(r'^-?\d+$', ans):
            numbers = re.findall(r'-?\d+', resp)
            is_correct = bool(numbers) and numbers[-1] == ans

    kbench.assertions.assert_true(is_correct,
        expectation=f"Expected '{answer}', got '{response.strip()[:200]}'. "
                    f"Type: {type}, Difficulty: {difficulty}")
    return is_correct
